params.plan_text:
Discharge plan for 73F with HTN (I10), long-term medication use (Z79.899), and complication of medical care (T88.7XXA).

ADVERSE EVENT DISCLOSURE: During this admission, an adverse event occurred on  (event_id ae-seeded-v2-safety_event-bgpr-004). This event has been formally documented, reported via institutional quality reporting (quality-1ac041179f10), and disclosed to the patient and family per patient safety and transparency policy.

Follow-up safety precautions:
1. Follow up with primary care physician within 7 days of discharge to review the adverse event, reconcile medications, and assess recovery.
2. Patient/family education provided regarding signs and symptoms requiring urgent evaluation; return precautions reviewed.
3. Continue home BP monitoring; report sustained BP >140/90 or symptoms.
4. Medication reconciliation completed; review long-term medications at follow-up.
5. Outpatient safety follow-up referral placed for ongoing monitoring related to the documented adverse event.
6. Patient provided written copy of adverse event disclosure and contact information for patient relations/risk management for any questions.

Full event chronology retained in the medical record.
